title: Creative Selection Strategy
description: How a creative agent samples which catalog items / signal conditions to produce creatives for when max_creatives is below the eligible count (#5262). Selection ordering surfaces on the existing rank/recommended fields of build_creative's creatives[].variants[]. This is a curated set, not an open extension point — new strategies are added per the enum-membership criterion in spec-guidelines.md. Part of the experimental signal-fanout surface (x-status: experimental; feature id `creative.signal_fanout`).

enumDescriptions.audience_relevance: Rank by relevance to the buyer's audience signal(s) — the user-side axis. Drawn from the SAME signal_ref pointers in signal_conditions / package targeting, not a parallel signals[] array.
enumDescriptions.contextual_fit: Rank by fit to the content/context the creative renders against — the content-side counterpart to audience_relevance (user-side). Uses the same signal_ref mechanism with a context signal rather than an audience signal. Mirrors the Trusted Match Protocol Context Match / Identity Match split.
enumDescriptions.performance: Rank by the catalog item's historical delivery performance (e.g. CTR, completion, conversion) where the agent has that signal.
enumDescriptions.proximity: Rank by proximity to a location input. Reuse the existing geo targeting shape (see docs/media-buy/advanced-topics/targeting.mdx) rather than a parallel one; the exact geo-input binding is still being settled, which the experimental status of this surface keeps revisable.
enumDescriptions.inventory_priority: Rank by seller-side catalog metadata (margin, overstock, promotional priority). No buyer input.
enumDescriptions.random: Status-quo fallback: agent samples without a ranking preference. Default when selection_strategy is omitted.
